anchor.snippet: A Banach space of dimension higher than 2 is (isometrically) a Hilbert space if and only if, for every closed subspace V

annotations.note: The Kakutani-type isometric characterization of Hilbert spaces by norm-1 projections is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Topological characterization via complementary subspaces

anchor.snippet: A Banach space X is topologically and linearly isomorphic to a Hilbert space if and only if

annotations.note: The Lindenstrauss–Tzafriri-type topological characterization is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Closure equals double orthogonal complement

anchor.snippet: the closure of a subspace can be completely characterized in terms of the orthogonal complement

annotations.note: `orthogonal_orthogonal_eq_closure` gives `K⊥⊥ = closure K` in a complete space.

anchors.snippet: the closure of a subspace can be completely characterized in terms of the orthogonal complement

annotations.label: Orthogonal projection theorem (box)
annotations.note: The projection theorem boxed in the article corresponds to the `orthogonalProjection` API including `exists_norm_eq_iInf_of_complete_subspace`.

annotations.label: Spectrum of an operator

anchor.snippet: The spectrum of an operator T , denoted σ ( T ) , is the set of complex numbers

annotations.note: `spectrum 𝕜 a` is the spectrum of an element of an algebra.

anchors.snippet: The spectrum of an operator T , denoted σ ( T ) , is the set of complex numbers

annotations.label: Spectrum of self-adjoint operators is real and bounded

anchor.snippet: If T is self-adjoint, then the spectrum is real

annotations.note: `IsSelfAdjoint.mem_spectrum_eq_re` shows spectrum of a self-adjoint element is real; boundedness follows from compactness of the spectrum in a Banach algebra.

anchors.snippet: If T is self-adjoint, then the spectrum is real

annotations.label: Spectral theorem for compact self-adjoint operators

anchor.snippet: A compact self-adjoint operator T has only countably (or finitely) many spectral values

annotations.note: Mathlib has the finite-dimensional diagonalization of symmetric operators; the compact-operator version is not stated.

anchors.snippet: A compact self-adjoint operator T has only countably (or finitely) many spectral values

annotations.label: Spectral theorem for bounded self-adjoint operators via spectral family

anchor.snippet: The general spectral theorem for self-adjoint operators involves a kind of operator-valued Riemann–Stieltjes integral

annotations.note: Continuous functional calculus is formalized; the resolution-of-the-identity / spectral-measure form is not.

anchors.snippet: The general spectral theorem for self-adjoint operators involves a kind of operator-valued Riemann–Stieltjes integral

annotations.label: Spectral mapping theorem

anchor.snippet: A major application of spectral methods is the spectral mapping theorem

annotations.note: Mathlib has the spectral mapping theorem for the continuous functional calculus and polynomials, but not in a single "spectral mapping theorem" form for arbitrary self-adjoint operators.

anchors.snippet: A major application of spectral methods is the spectral mapping theorem

annotations.label: Spectral theorem for unbounded self-adjoint operators (box)
annotations.note: The unbounded spectral theorem (resolution of identity for unbounded self-adjoint operators) is not in Mathlib.
